title: Essay on Shinylive
text:
Shinylive is a new way to run Shiny applications directly in the browser, without a server.
This is possible because R code is compiled to WebAssembly and executed client‑side.
In other words:

Traditional Shiny = requires an R server

Shinylive = runs entirely in the user’s browser

This makes Shiny apps easier to share, embed, and publish.

title: Essay on Shinylive
text:
✔️ Pros of Shinylive

No server required

Free hosting

Easy to share

Great for teaching, demos, and lightweight apps

title: Essay on Shinylive
text:
❗ Cons of Shinylive

Not ideal for large datasets

Browser memory limits

Slower for heavy computations

Fewer UI customization options

title: Welcome
text:
Welcome to my Module 10 website for MSDM. I am a Digital Marketing Master’s student at Cal Poly Pomona with a passion for blending creativity, analytics, and audience engagement.


I am pursuing a career in integrated marketing within the sports and entertainment industry, where I hope to combine data‑driven strategy, fan engagement, and creative storytelling to elevate brand experiences.



I bring hands‑on experience across marketing, events, and customer engagement, including:

Event operations at the Rose Bowl Stadium
Aquatics marketing & HR support at the Rose Bowl Aquatics Center
Brand ambassador campaigns for Farmer John and Disney x Brooks
Digital marketing analytics and GA4 reporting
Creative content development and presentation design

I enjoy turning insights into strategy and crafting meaningful experiences for audiences.

title: Dashboard
text:
Dashboard Code & Output
Below is the embedded dashboard from my CEP project.

title: Dashboard
text:
📊 Trust Item for Second Visualization

trust_items_long <- trust_clean %>%
  select(condition, starts_with("trust_")) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("trust_"), names_to = "item", values_to = "score") %>%
  left_join(trust_labels, by = "item")

title: Dashboard
section: 📝 Executive Summary - What This Means for MEin3D
text:
📝 Executive Summary - What This Means for MEin3D

The dashboard shows that AI‑generated email content performs on par with human‑written messaging in terms of trust. Customers rate both emails as similarly trustworthy, meaning MEin3D can safely adopt AI‑powered personalization without risking customer confidence. This supports shifting toward automated, scalable email workflows that reduce production time while maintaining strong customer perception.

title: Essay on Shiny Apps
text:
Shiny is an R package that allows analysts and marketers to build interactive web applications directly from R. Unlike static dashboards, Shiny apps let users explore data dynamically through filters, inputs, and real‑time updates.


A Shiny app has two main components:


Defines what the user sees:

Dropdown menus

Sliders

Plots

Tables

Text outputs




Defines how the app responds:

Reads user inputs

Runs R code dynamically

Updates visualizations in real time

Together, the UI and server create an interactive experience without requiring JavaScript or web development skills.

title: Essay on Shiny Apps
text:
Summary
Shiny apps are ideal when: - You want interactivity - Your audience needs to explore data - You want to build tools without learning JavaScript
They are a key skill for modern data‑driven marketing teams.
